name: Natural Gas / LNG
summary: Day 85 (Saturday May 23 — markets closed). **HENRY HUB NATURAL GAS $2.91 MAY 22 CLOSE (-3.7% 1D, +11.2% MoM, -2.6% 3M) — FRIDAY'S DROP = EIA STORAGE REPORT (92 Bcf INJECTION IN-LINE, BUILDS SURPLUS); 1M PREMIUM = LNG-BASIS STORY.** Per markets dossier C8: EIA weekly storage report Thursday showed 92 Bcf injection in line with 5Y-average, holding the +140 Bcf surplus to seasonal norm for the third consecutive week (Natural Gas Intelligence) → Friday session priced through storage-comfortable read → -3.7% sell-off. Henry Hub close $2.91 below the 2026 expected average of $3.50. **The +11.2% 1M is the LNG-basis story**: Qatar Ras Laffan force majeure (March 2 with 17% liquefaction loss persistent) + tightness in European LNG supply spread → US LNG export demand bid → 1M Henry Hub differential vs storage-implied price. **TTF Gas €48.69/MWh May 22 close** (-1.46% 1D, +2.1% MoM, +30%+ vs ~€31 baseline) — flat-to-easing; Qatar Ras Laffan FM 17% liquefaction persists but Norway maintenance ease holding. **Cheniere Energy (LNG) $240.85** (+0.2% 1D, -6.1% 1M, +8.1% 3M) — LNG-basis structural bid intact. **Friday's -3.7% is a domestic storage signal; the +11.2% 1M is a crisis-linked LNG-basis signal. Both correct interpretations at their respective timeframes.** Qatar Ras Laffan Trains 4/6 (12.8 mtpa, ~17% Qatar LNG) still 3-5yr recovery. PRIOR (May 19): **Henry Hub $3.04/MMBtu (+2.8% 1D, +13.8% 1M, +1.1% 3M)** — Summer cooling + Qatar LNG complex still 20% offline; sympathy with TTF. **B4 European TTF Gas €50.16/MWh — flat versus Friday €50.85 close** (within daily noise); past month +18.26%; +42.75% YoY; ~20% of global LNG supply disrupted since war began. Friday's +6.7% one-day move NOT extending Monday — gas complex treating maximal-pressure weekend tape as already priced. Cheniere Energy (LNG) $241.84 +0.3% 1D (-7.7% 1M, +10.7% 3M); Cheniere CCO 'astounded prices not higher' framing carries forward. Heating Oil $3.98 -1.9% 1D / +17.1% 1M / +58.0% 3M (refiner slate consolidating after +58% 3M distillate ramp). RBOB Gasoline $3.59 -3.1% 1D (counter-trend after +82% 3M). **Qatar Ras Laffan Trains 4/6 (12.8 mtpa, ~17% Qatar LNG) still 3-5yr recovery — physical Qatar LNG outage continues to provide structural floor.** Hormuz functionally throttled at ~30 ships/day per Aramco (partially explained by Iran-permitted Chinese selective transit). **Brent $111.47 +2.0% Monday (StanChart $130-145 path activates — 2-session sustain rule met)** — natural-gas-LNG bid carries Brent's risk premium via energy complex linkage. **PRIOR (May 16): Henry Hub $2.96/MMBtu (+2.3% 1D, +11.8% 1M, -2.3% 3M) — LNG-global tightness pull (TTF €49.86) + Hormuz LNG dislocation** (~70% crisis). **B4 TTF €49.86/MWh +4.63% 1D, third consecutive daily increase and second-consecutive >1% move; highest since April** — driver chain ongoing Qatar Ras Laffan force majeure on ~17% of liquefaction capacity + Hormuz tensions + Europe pre-winter storage rebuild. **Cheniere (LNG) $241.84 +0.3% 1D / -7.7% 1M / +10.7% 3M** — long-term LNG hedged at pre-crisis prices remains the headwind; equity-multiple compression on crisis-unwind expectation continues even as physical complex tightens. Heating Oil $4.05 +3.8% 1D / +69.6% 3M (oil pass-through, distillate-tight slate persists). RBOB Gasoline $3.70 +2.7% 1D / +17.0% 1M (refiner slate shift toward distillates + Brent extension + summer-driving prep). **Qatar Ras Laffan Trains 4/6 (12.8 mtpa, ~17% Qatar LNG) still 3-5yr recovery — physical Qatar LNG outage continues to provide structural floor.** Hormuz functionally throttled at ~30 ships/day per Aramco (partially explained by Iran-permitted Chinese selective transit). ECB Lagarde 'new energy shock' May 6 framing carries forward into the regime read. PRIOR (May 14): **Henry Hub $2.89/MMBtu (+0.8% 1D, +10.6% 1M, -4.8% 3M)** — Hormuz-LNG bid spillover via TTF arbitrage; Cheniere supply pricing pulling US gas higher (~80% crisis). **TTF €46.63/MWh May 13 (-0.11% 1D, basically flat at +47% from pre-war €31.20)** — sustained-elevated mode, neither alarm nor relief (B4). **C3 Cheniere (LNG) $239.38 (-2.0% 1D, -7.6% 1M, +9.6% 3M)** = $3.5bn Q1 hedging derivatives loss from Iran-war price blowout + LNG infrastructure trade-unwind on diminishing Hormuz-tail-risk + Trump-Xi opening = US LNG-to-China future demand normalization (positive long-term but reduces crisis-premium short-term). **The Q1 hedging loss is a one-time print but the equity-multiple compression is the crisis-unwind signal.** Hormuz functionally throttled — Aramco CEO Nasser line carries forward (2-5 ships/day, 100M bbl/week lost). Qatar Ras Laffan Trains 4/6 (12.8 mtpa, ~17% Qatar LNG) still 3-5yr recovery. **B4 TTF €46.63 + Container freight FBX13 stable = confirms quiet European-energy-channel.** PRIOR (May 13): TTF €46.23/MWh May 11 (+4.74% 1D, +5% from May 8 €44 baseline, +31.57% YoY) — stepped up on Trump rejection + Aramco 2-5 ships/day Hormuz deterioration. **Cheniere LNG $240.70 May 12 (+0.5% 1D, -9.1% 1M, +10.8% 3M) — Q1 2026 $3.5B GAAP loss on derivative MtM** still in price; CCO Anatol Feygin (May 7): 'astounded that global gas prices have not risen higher with the closure of the Strait of Hormuz disrupting 100 cargoes a month.' US LNG players are SHORT the war via hedge books. **Hormuz functionally throttled — Aramco CEO Nasser May 11: 2-5 ships/day (DETERIORATED from May 10 ~6/day), 100M bbl/week lost, 880M cumulative, market won't normalize until 2027 if disruption persists past mid-June.** Qatar Ras Laffan Trains 4/6 (12.8 mtpa, ~17% Qatar LNG) still 3-5yr recovery. **NEW VARIABLE:** WSJ May 11 revealed UAE secretly struck Iran's Lavan Island oil refinery April 8 (55,000 bpd offline months) — affects oil specifically, not LNG, but reframes Gulf-state covert military participation. Helium node update: Korean fab buffer ~4 weeks remaining to mid-June exhaustion. **Reversibility:** TTF/HH partial decoupling continues; Cheniere -9.1% 1M reflects markets pricing-through peace optionality despite operational tightness. Qatar Ras Laffan reopening is in any MOU framework but physical recovery years out regardless of diplomatic resolution.

current.delta_1m: HH +3.3%; TTF -25%+ from March peak; Cheniere -9.7%

recent_signals.headline: **CHENIERE +2.4% — EU STORAGE 141 CARGOES BELOW 5-YEAR LOW + RAISED 2026 EBITDA GUIDE TO $7.25-7.75B; HORMUZ LNG BASIS STRUCTURAL.** European TTF €50.17/MWh holds elevated; Hormuz-LNG-dislocation re-priced US LNG basis structurally. **US LNG fungibility into European-emergency-supply is structurally bid; Hormuz reopening is bearish for this trade, but reopen-timeline says 2H26.** Henry Hub $3.02 (-0.3% 1D); +12.1% 1M (LNG export pull + Cheniere demand; partially crisis-driven via European basis). **Cheniere Energy as potential new node**: appears in 4+ independent sources as transmission mechanism between European TTF ↔ Hormuz LNG dislocation ↔ US LNG export.
recent_signals.verification: Cheniere +2.4% = CONFIRMED operational; $7.25-7.75B EBITDA guide = CONFIRMED; EU storage 141 cargoes below 5y low = REPORTED (Cheniere management); Henry Hub flat = CONFIRMED.

recent_signals.headline: SUNDAY HOLIDAY — TTF ~€44/MWh weekly (-25%+ from March €60+ peak, +40%+ vs Feb ~€31 baseline); Cheniere $240.11 May 8 close (-2.7% 1D, -9.7% 1M, +11.3% 3M); Q1 $3.5B GAAP loss on derivative MtM (long-term hedges struck pre-war at lower TTF/HH spread became deep underwater after Qatar force majeure spiked TTF €60+ in March). Cheniere CCO Anatol Feygin: 'astounded that global gas prices have not risen higher with the closure of the Strait of Hormuz disrupting 100 cargoes a month.' 2026 adjusted EBITDA guidance RAISED to $7.25-7.75B (from $6.75-7.25B). HEADLINE GAAP LOSS IS NON-CASH; CASH EARNINGS POWER INCREASED. **Sunday May 10 bulk-carrier-near-Doha strike is operationally adjacent to Ras Laffan — Qatar's main LNG/helium export complex.** If strike location was deliberate, signals graduated escalation against Qatari LNG export infrastructure rather than against US-blockade-facilitating shipping. Qatar Ras Laffan Trains 4/6 (12.8 mtpa, ~17% Qatar LNG) still 3-5yr recovery. Cheniere is cleanest US-LNG-exporter beneficiary of any extended Qatar disruption.
sources: Bloomberg (Cheniere CCO comment + Q1 derivative loss)
UKMTO Warning notice (bulk carrier near Doha)
recent_signals.verification: CONFIRMED prices yfinance multi-source; Cheniere Q1 derivative loss = CONFIRMED Bloomberg primary; Cheniere CCO Hormuz comment = CONFIRMED public; bulk carrier strike near Ras Laffan = CONFIRMED ACTION (UKMTO primary); deliberate-vs-coincidental strike location = OPEN question

recent_signals.headline: BRANCH 3 → CHENIERE EUROPEAN-ENERGY-EMERGENCY PREMIUM COMPRESSION; HH +6.8% NON-CRISIS

historical_summaries.summary: 4 signals. QatarEnergy halted LNG production at Ras Laffan (world's largest LNG facility, ~20% of global supply) after attacks | Iranian missiles damaged LNG Trains 4 & 6 at Ras Laffan | European TTF surged 70% in March to EUR54
18 signals. Eurozone inflation surged to 2 | CONFIRMED: European TTF gas at EUR 47 | CONFIRMED: Heating oil +3
